actionUnits.condition: Target MonoBehaviour (id: 939) on GameObject 'MessageText' (fileID: 130) maps to script GUID 6e888d82ad62a4fd99699eeee0553755, which corresponds to ShowMessage. ShowMessage exposes one public entry point RunMessage(string) that starts a coroutine to set the UI Text and fade the parent CanvasGroup in, wait 5s, then fade out. Start() will auto-run on scene load to cache Text and CanvasGroup. However: (1) MessageText has no Rigidbody, Collider, or EventTrigger; (2) There are no UnityEvents (m_Calls) configured in the scene; (3) The only public method requires a string parameter, while this test framework only allows parameterless method calls. Therefore we cannot legally invoke RunMessage under current constraints. To achieve coverage, we need either: a) a configured UnityEvent (Button/Interactable) that invokes ShowMessage.RunMessage with a test string; or b) allowance to pass a string parameter to RunMessage; or c) an additional parameterless wrapper method on the component (e.g., RunHello) that internally calls RunMessage("Hello VR"); and d) confirm the parent has a CanvasGroup and its initial alpha is 0 so the fade-in path is exercised. This Trigger reserves 7.5s to cover ~1s fade-in + 5s wait + ~1s fade-out once RunMessage is invocable.
